← the Atlas

pulam-web — the browser twin (kolu's surface-consumption leg, proven standalone)

Features· seedling ·proposed ·JUN 22, 2026

A browser ↔ Node ↔ ssh app (drishti's twin for the terminal-workspace surface) that proves kolu's exact browser-consumption leg before remote-terminals R8b — a live git-status view reaching a browser over websocketLink + surfaceClient + Solid reconcile, sourced from a mirrored pulam, auto-provisioned over ssh. Node + Vite, matching kolu's own client stack (the surface leg is identical @kolu/surface code, so it is a faithful reproduction of kolu's leg). Lands cheapest-first — Step 0 graduates the reactive stream consumer in drishti; R4.8a stands up the whole framework (provision · fan-out · mirror · re-serve) rendering only a terminal list; R4.8b layers the user-facing features (live git status, drill-in).

Contents

  • User-facing description
  • Architecture-level changes
  • Implementation details
    • Step 0 (in drishti) — graduate the reactive stream consumer
    • R4.8a — the framework (the whole pipeline, rendering only a terminal list)
    • R4.8b — the user-facing features (the R8b proof + the product)
  • History

pulam-web is drishti’s twin, pointed at the terminal-workspace surface. Where drishti monitors per-host processes in a browser, pulam-web monitors per-host terminals — the fleet you already see in pulam-tui, but in a browser, auto-provisioned over ssh. It exists for one engineering reason on top of being a real product: it is kolu’s own browser-consumption leg, minus kolu — websocketLink → surfaceClient → Solid reconcile — so it graduates the path remote-terminals R8b is blocked on, in a build whose console you can actually see.

User-facing description

pulam.local — every terminal, every host⟳ live
hostslocalnix@prod sshstaging copying…+ host
▌ zest · 3
●claudekolu · feat/dial-ssh●2✎3↑2
◜codexdrishti · master✓ clean
◜claudeanyforge · fix/checks✎1?4↓1
▌ nix@prod · 2 · ssh
◜claudeinfra · deploy✎2
○—notes · main✓ clean
kolu · feat/dial-ssh
↑2 ahead of origin/feat/dial-ssh
●2 staged✎3 modified
A packages/server/src/ssh.ts
M packages/server/src/wire.ts
M packages/client/src/Code.tsx
M docs/atlas/.../pulam.mdx
M flake.nix
↵ open · esc close · live

Leave it open on a second monitor: a fleet of every terminal across every host, grouped by host, each row a live git-status cell (●staged ✎modified ?untracked ↑ahead↓behind). Click a row and a drill-in pane shows that repo’s full status — branch tracking, the staged/modified/untracked split, and the changed-file list — exactly pulam-tui’s view, in a browser. Hosts are added on demand and auto-provisioned over ssh (the copying… chip is the live provision state), so a teammate opens one URL and watches the whole fleet. It is git status, never file content — the changed-file list, not bodies or diffs (the same scope as R4.7).

Architecture-level changes

It is the same @kolu/surface code kolu and drishti run — that is the whole point. Serve side: implementSurface → oRPC RPCHandler (@orpc/server/ws) → handler.upgrade(ws) on a /rpc/ws socket. Consume side: websocketLink → surfaceClient/surfaceClients → the reactive .use() hooks (surfaceClient.ts owns snapshot-then-deltas + reconcile). drishti proves this trio is electricity; kolu’s Code tab proves .streams.use() for a value-bearing gitStatus stream. pulam-web is the first standalone app to put those together — value-bearing git status, over .streams.use(), sourced from a mirror — which is precisely R8b’s path.

Node, matching kolu’s own stack. The surface leg is identical @kolu/surface code on any runtime — drishti even writes its server Node-style (@hono/node-server + the ws package) and runs it on Bun only for its Bun.build client bundler. We go Node, like kolu-server, and bundle the SolidJS frontend with Vite + vite-plugin-solid (kolu’s own client toolchain) rather than drishti’s Bun.build — so pulam-web is kolu’s stack end-to-end.

Common it up — what graduates to the surface family, what stays app-local (grounded)

The directive was don’t replicate drishti’s plumbing into pulam-web — upstream the common stuff. Grounding both apps against the installed code drew the line precisely:

  • What graduates — the per-host mirror-bridge + registry → @kolu/surface-nix-host. drishti’s bridgeAgentToParent (a makeClientCursor reconnect loop re-issuing mirrorRemoteSurface per spawn + a live-procedure forwarding holder) and its hostRegistry (Map<host,{session,handler}>) are ~600 lines pulam-web would otherwise copy verbatim. They graduate as pumpRemoteSurface + buildHostRegistry — the N-host, consume-side companions to nix-host’s existing makeClientCursor + getHostSession, with zero new deps (the registry stays generic over the handler type, so no @orpc/server/ws in nix-host). Both apps consume one copy.
  • What stays app-local — the ws-serve upgrade block. It is not the right thing to extract: @kolu/surface core has no ws dep (putting a WebSocketServer helper there inverts its transport-agnostic boundary — a defect per electricity), and drishti’s real handler validates ?host= and destroy()s the raw socket before handleUpgrade, plus dispatches an __admin__ sentinel to a second handler — neither fits a generic onConnection(ws) seam. So each app keeps its ~18-line upgrade block, composed from the already-shared gateWsOrigin (@kolu/surface/ws-origin) + gateStaleSocket + startWsHeartbeat (@kolu/surface-app/server).

So the extraction is drishti’s own proven code lifted up — drishti’s adoption is a near-mechanical swap whose green CI proves the lift was behaviour-preserving (the same way gateStaleSocket was already extracted from drishti).

The design fork R8b rides on — resolve it here, grounded

Two ways git status reaches a browser. Design A (what #1510 did, and stuck): ship terminal-workspace’s raw {seq} pulse to the browser and re-query getStatus client-side via useWatchedRead. Design B (what koluSurface already does — packages/server/src/surface.ts:271-277, {read:getStatus, install:subscribeRepoChange, isEqual} — and the Code tab proves daily): wrap it into one value-bearing stream server-side; the browser consumes an ordinary value-bearing stream via .streams.use(). Lead: Design B. For a remote host the backend wraps the mirrored subscribeRepoChange + forwarded getStatus (R7) into that value-bearing stream — so the raw {seq} pulse never crosses to the browser, and the “ungraduated primitive” dissolves as an artifact of A. pulam-web confirms B end-to-end from a mirrored source; that becomes R8b’s recipe.

Implementation details

Three steps, each isolating one risk. Step 0 (in drishti) graduates the reactive stream consumer — kolu’s failing leg — on its own. R4.8a stands up the entire framework (provision · dial · fan-out · mirror · re-serve · browser-consume) rendering only a terminal list — no features, so a plumbing failure has nowhere to hide. R4.8b layers the user-facing features (live git status, drill-in), combining R4.8a’s proven framework with Step 0’s proven consumer. Step 0 and R4.8a are independent (one’s a drishti change, the other a fresh app); R4.8b needs both; R8b gates on R4.8b.

Step 0 (in drishti) — graduate the reactive stream consumer

Before pulam-web exists, prove its riskiest piece — .streams.use() + Solid reconcile for a mirrored, value-bearing stream — where the browser ⇄ ssh ⇄ mirror stack already runs: drishti. drishti’s processesSnapshot (a value-bearing snapshot-then-delta stream it mirrors per host, packages/common/src/surface.ts:279) is consumed today imperatively — streamCall(app.rpc.surface.processesSnapshot.get) + for await + a teardown controller (packages/app/src/client/App.tsx:749,879). Switch that one call site to app.streams.processesSnapshot.use(() => ({})) + render .value(). That proves the exact R8b consumer — a mirrored value-bearing stream → re-served → browser .streams.use() + reconcile — in a real browser with a visible console, and deletes the hand-rolled controller. It is the R7 pattern again (drishti graduated forwarded procedures via “Kill process”; here it graduates the reactive stream consumer via its process list) — a genuine drishti improvement (declarative replacing imperative), not a throwaway. It de-risks the consumer; the git-status wrap (Design B) and the fleet UI stay pulam-web’s. A drishti PR — independent of R4.8a’s framework, and the thing R4.8b’s git-status consumer rides on.

R4.8a — the framework (the whole pipeline, rendering only a terminal list)

R4.8a stands up everything hard — auto-provision + dial N pulam over ssh, mirror each, fan out, re-serve to the browser over /rpc/ws, and consume in the browser — but renders only a plain list of terminals (grouped by host). No git status, no drill-in, no features. The point is to prove the plumbing with a payload so small a failure has nowhere to hide.

  • Backend (Node) — per host, buildEntry(host): getHostSession({ binary:"pulam", resolveDrvPath, … }) (the pooled, reconnecting session — getHostSession + makeClientCursor, not one-shot dialAgentOnce, so a transient ssh drop self-heals), a per-host re-serve of the whole terminalWorkspaceSurface via implementSurface (implementSurface fail-fast-throws on any unimplemented primitive, so the re-serve folds version + awareness from the mirror and forwards the rest — fs.*/git.* procedures via the live-procedure holder, the activity/repo/file streams via the live client — never a degraded stub), and pumpRemoteSurface(session, makeSink) (the shared reconnect-mirror loop) folding the agent’s frames in. The N entries live in buildHostRegistry({ buildEntry }); one RPCHandler.upgrade(ws) per /rpc/ws?host=<id>, dispatched by an app-local upgrade block (gateWsOrigin + gateStaleSocket + startWsHeartbeat, all already shared). pulam-tui’s hostConnect.ts is the dial template.
  • Browser (SolidJS + Vite) — per host a surfaceClient(terminalWorkspaceSurface, websocketLink(ws?host=<id>)) → app.collections.awareness.use({}) → render a list of terminals per host (cwd · foreground · agent), plus a coarse connecting… overlay until the first frame. This is the .collections.use() consumer (which drishti already proves); the value-bearing stream consumer (.streams.use()) is deferred to R4.8b. (For R4.8a the host roster is a static /api/hosts boot list; dynamic add + the fine copying… provision state ride a small parent fleet surface in a follow-up — terminalWorkspaceSurface itself carries no connection cell.)

Done when: the browser shows a live terminal list across N auto-provisioned pulam hosts — its terminals appear and come/go live (awareness deltas) — all over the real ws. The mirror → re-serve → browser-store path is proven deterministically by the hermetic test (below). No features yet. That alone proves the framework: provision · dial · reconnect · fan-out · ws-serve · browser collection-consume.

R4.8b — the user-facing features (the R8b proof + the product)

With R4.8a’s framework proven and Step 0’s stream consumer proven, R4.8b just layers the features onto the pipeline:

  • Live git status — wrap subscribeRepoChange + getStatus into a value-bearing gitStatus stream server-side (Design B), consume it via .streams.use() + reconcile (the Step 0-proven hook — not pulam-tui’s raw mirrorRemoteSurface loop, not drishti’s for await), and paint the row’s git cell. This is the leg R8b rides on.
  • Drill-in + the polished UI — port pulam-tui’s pure gitCell / gitDetail projections (render.ts) to web DOM; selection + the drill-in pane (the mockup above). The RepoWatchSet + repo-membership reconcile from pulam-tui/src/fleet.ts carry over (renderer-agnostic — fleet.ts:6-7) via a FleetSink over a Solid createStore; only the renderer is new.

The trap — three consumers, only one is kolu's

pulam-tui reads streams by raw mirrorRemoteSurface iteration; drishti reads streams by raw for await over streamCall. Neither exercises the .streams.use() + Solid reconcile hook — the path kolu’s Code tab uses and #1510 stuck on. Copy drishti for the backend mirror + provision + ws-serve and for cells/collections (.use()), but the git-status stream must go through .streams.use() + reconcile, value-bearing (Design B) — that consumer is the reason pulam-web exists.

Reuse map (grounded against installed code in this worktree + /home/srid/code/drishti). Shared = consumed from the surface family, not copied; app-local = pulam-web’s own; R4.8b = not needed for the framework.

ConcernSourceNote
per-host mirror loopshared @kolu/surface-nix-host pumpRemoteSurfaceextracted from drishti bridgeAgentToParent; makeClientCursor reconnect + mirrorRemoteSurface per spawn + live-procedure/live-client holders
fan-out registryshared @kolu/surface-nix-host buildHostRegistryextracted from drishti hostRegistry.ts; Map<host,{session,handler}>, generic over the handler type
ws-serve (~18 lines)app-local in pulam-webcomposed from shared gateWsOrigin (@kolu/surface/ws-origin) + gateStaleSocket + startWsHeartbeat (@kolu/surface-app/server); ?host= dispatch is the app’s (see the common-it-up callout above)
whole-surface re-serveapp-local implementSurface(terminalWorkspaceSurface, …)folds version+awareness from the mirror, forwards fs.*/git.* + the watcher streams (fail-fast: every primitive implemented)
autoprovision + dialshared @kolu/surface-nix-host getHostSession (+ resolveSystem)dial template = pulam-tui/src/hostConnect.ts; pins binary:"pulam", PULAM_AGENT_DRVS_JSON. Provisioning lives inside the session’s reconnect cycle — no hand-rolled provisionAgent call
browser bootstrapdrishti app/src/client/wire.ts (pattern)one surfaceClient(terminalWorkspaceSurface, websocketLink(ws?host=)) per host (@kolu/surface/solid + @kolu/surface/links/websocket)
serve the bundleshared installFreshStatic (@kolu/surface-app/server)the same fresh-static contract kolu-server uses
fleet.ts / render.ts (gitCell, gitDetail, RepoWatchSet)pulam-tui — R4.8bgit cells + drill-in; renderer-agnostic core ports then, not now
bundler + nixVite + vite-plugin-solid (kolu’s packages/client toolchain), Nodein-repo package: @kolu/* are workspace deps (no npins); nix derivation + flake/justfile wiring modeled on packages/client

The hermetic test. No in-memory websocketLink double exists (only directLink/stdio/unix-socket). Model on R7’s mirrorRemoteSurface.test.ts: stand up a real terminalWorkspaceSurface agent via implementSurface, connect it with directLink, drive pulam-web’s buildReServe fold path (the mirror sink) with that client, then consume the re-served surface through a second surfaceClient + a Solid reconcile store — asserting the awareness keys re-notify on the second delta (agent → mirror → re-serve → browser-store). buildReServe is split so the mirror step takes an injected client, which is exactly what makes it driveable without ssh. That catches a reconcile break deterministically; a ws-flush break only shows over a real socket — so pulam-web over real ws is the full proof, the unit test necessary-but-not-sufficient.

Definition of done — by phase. Step 0 (drishti): processesSnapshot rendered live via .streams.use(), its hand-rolled controller deleted. R4.8a: a live terminal list across N auto-provisioned hosts over the real ws — the framework, no features. R4.8b: a live git-status cell + drill-in updating within ~1s of a working-tree change (video/screenshot over the real ws), the consumer confirmed in code as .streams.use() + reconcile, value-bearing, the hermetic test green, and the adopted design recorded as R8b’s recipe.

Shared @kolu/surface* — the drishti gate APPLIES this round

@kolu/surface, @kolu/surface-app, @kolu/surface-nix-host are shared with drishti, and any API-facing change to them requires a linked drishti PR with green CI (.claude/rules/surface.md). The common-it-up extraction adds two exports to @kolu/surface-nix-host — pumpRemoteSurface + buildHostRegistry — so the gate is in scope (additive, non-breaking, but API-facing). The linked drishti PR refactors drishti’s bridgeAgentToParent + hostRegistry.ts to consume the shared helpers (dropping its hand-rolled copies), bumps drishti’s npins kolu pin to this branch, and goes green — its passing CI is what proves the lift behaviour-preserving. pulam-web’s consumption of the rest (mirrorRemoteSurface, getHostSession, gateStaleSocket, …) stays read-only.

History

  • R4.8a grounded + building (2026-06-22) — deep-grounded the framework against the installed code (drishti’s bridgeAgentToParent/hostRegistry, the @kolu/surface* family, pulam-tui’s dial, the terminalWorkspaceSurface shape, kolu’s Vite/nix client toolchain). Two corrections to the original sketch, both load-bearing: (1) the common-it-up extraction is the per-host mirror-bridge + registry (pumpRemoteSurface + buildHostRegistry → @kolu/surface-nix-host), not a ws-serve helper — surface core has no ws dep and drishti’s upgrade handler doesn’t fit a generic seam, so ws-serve stays app-local from already-shared gates; (2) the per-host re-serve is the whole terminalWorkspaceSurface (fail-fast on any omission), folding version+awareness and forwarding the rest, dialed via the reconnecting getHostSession+makeClientCursor (not one-shot dialAgentOnce). The shared extraction landed first (typecheck + 11 registry tests green); the drishti adoption rides a linked gated PR.
  • Planned (2026-06-22) — branched out of pulam’s R4.8 once it grew its own UI and a layered plan (Step 0 in drishti · R4.8a framework · R4.8b features). Grounded against /home/srid/code/drishti (the surface leg is identical @kolu/surface code, so the runtime is free — chose Node + Vite to match kolu-server/client; Bun was only drishti’s bundler; autoprovision is getHostSession+provisionAgent) and against pulam-tui (the startFleet/FleetSink/RepoWatchSet core is renderer-agnostic and reusable). Gates remote-terminals R8b.

Referenced by

  • pulam — the terminal-workspace surface as an ephemeral daemon on kaval
  • Remote terminals over SSH
